subject: Gaming
title: Hironobu Sakaguchi: Final Fantasy's Father
description: A 60-second lesson on Hironobu Sakaguchi: Final Fantasy's Father.

hook.text: Sakaguchi named it Final Fantasy because he was ready to quit gaming. It saved his career instead.

buildup.text: After multiple flops at Square, Sakaguchi poured everything into one last game in 1987.

discovery.text: Final Fantasy combined story, exploration, and strategy into a formula that defined Japanese RPGs.

twist.text: The franchise made billions, but Sakaguchi left Square after the Final Fantasy movie lost $94 million.

climax.text: He continued making games independently. Final Fantasy now spans over 50 titles across four decades.

punchline.text: His 'final' game launched a 50-title empire. Never quit on your last try.

quiz.question: Why was the game named Final Fantasy?
options: Sakaguchi was planning to quit gaming and it was his last attempt
It was set at the end of the world
The publisher chose the name randomly
